label: Uncomplicated rib contusion without fracture or cardiopulmonary red flags
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated rib/chest wall contusion.
No fracture concern, pneumothorax/pulmonary injury concern, cardiac/PE concern, hypoxia, hemoptysis, syncope, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Fracture/dislocation concern, high-energy trauma, pneumothorax or pulmonary injury concern, hypoxia, hemoptysis, cardiac/PE concern, syncope, fever/systemic illness, severe pain, or unstable vitals.
Pregnancy/pediatric pathway or specialist-directed trauma/orthopedic plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care or urgent care if pain or breathing comfort is not improving.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports bruise/contusion framing, local care concepts, and escalation when bruising is severe, unusual, or associated with concerning symptoms. Autonomous Phase 2001-2100 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed rib/chest wall contusion without fracture concern, cardiopulmonary red flags, high-risk trauma, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. no fracture concern, cardiopulmonary red flags, high-risk trauma, or specialist-directed plan documented
